food_subgroup: Plant-based milk tea / fruit tea beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term exists for oat-milk mango jasmine tea beverage. FOODON_03301710 (fruit beverage) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (Fruit nectars and drinks) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for oat-milk mango jasmine tea blends.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 (Fruit drink) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code for oat-milk mango jasmine tea exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of approximately 400 ml, as served at HeyTea Singapore, containing oat milk, mango, and jasmine tea with cane sugar at default sweetness level.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: OATSIDE Oat Milk (USDA Branded Foods), Mango raw (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy), Jasmine Tea brewed (USDA FoodData Central), and cane sugar (USDA SR Legacy). Weighted blend estimate for a typical mango oat milk tea beverage.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy / Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for HeyTea Oat Mango Boom exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of component ingredients: approximately 50% oat milk, 25% mango puree, 20% brewed jasmine tea, and 5% cane sugar by volume, then diluted to per-100g basis. This is a medium-confidence estimate. Values should be verified against the brand's official nutrition panel when available.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of oat milk and mango-based beverages estimated from analogous foods: oat milk GI ~58 (Wolever et al., 2003, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition) and mango GI ~51 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al., 2008). Weighted blend estimate yields GI ~55.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HeyTea Oat Mango Boom. GI estimated as a weighted average of oat milk (~58, Wolever et al. 2003, Am J Clin Nutr) and mango (~51, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Jasmine tea contributes negligible glycemic load. Added cane sugar (sucrose GI ~65) raises the blended estimate to approximately 55. A GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 40.8) / 100 = 22.4. Serving carbohydrate = 10.2 g/100g × 400g = 40.8g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~18 mg per 100g; ~72 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 72 mg sodium per standard 400 ml serving, this beverage poses minimal sodium burden for individuals managing hypertension, though the added cane sugar content should be monitored as part of overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and high glycemic load per serving (~22.4 GL units)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this beverage: the default preparation contains cane sugar yielding approximately 31 g of sugar per 400 ml serving and a glycemic load of ~22.4, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; requesting no-sugar-added preparation substantially reduces glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar content and moderate energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The default cane-sugar preparation contributes approximately 208 kcal per 400 ml serving primarily from carbohydrates and sugar, which may contribute to excess caloric intake and dyslipidaemia risk in individuals with cardiovascular disease; opting for no-sugar-added preparation and consuming occasionally is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oat Mango Boom is a plant-based chilled beverage sold by HeyTea in collaboration with OATSIDE, a Singapore-based oat milk brand, available at HeyTea outlets in Singapore including Marina Bay Sands. The drink combines fresh or pureed mango, OATSIDE oat milk, and brewed jasmine tea, sweetened by default with real cane sugar. It is entirely dairy-free and vegan-suitable, making it accessible to lactose-intolerant consumers and those following plant-based diets. The flavour profile is fruity and tropical, with the natural sweetness and acidity of mango balanced by the creamy, slightly nutty character of oat milk and the floral fragrance of jasmine tea. The texture is smooth and lightly creamy. Nutritionally, the beverage provides moderate energy (~52 kcal per 100g; ~208 kcal per 400 ml serving) primarily from carbohydrates and natural sugars, with low protein (~0.6 g/100g) and low fat (~1.0 g/100g). The oat milk component contributes small amounts of dietary fibre, calcium, and beta-glucan. The default sugar preparation results in a glycemic load of approximately 22.4 per serving, which is clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Customers are advised to request no-sugar-added or reduced-sugar preparation to lower glycemic impact. The beverage contains oats and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or severe gluten sensitivity. It is classified as a processed plant-based fruit-tea beverage in the dessert-drinks category.
common_user_queries: Does HeyTea Oat Mango Boom contain dairy?
How many calories in HeyTea Oat Mango Boom?
Is Oat Mango Boom suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of Oat Mango Boom?
Is Oat Mango Boom gluten free?
Can I order Oat Mango Boom without sugar?
What is the glycemic index of mango oat milk tea?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for HeyTea Oat Mango Boom exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of component ingredients using USDA FoodData Central data for oat milk, raw mango, brewed jasmine tea, and cane sugar. GI is estimated from analogous foods in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium pending official brand nutrition panel verification.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-blend estimates based on USDA FoodData Central data; no official HeyTea or OATSIDE nutrition panel was available from approved sources at time of processing. Values should be updated when the brand publishes verified nutrition information. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero by formulation — plant-based oat milk and mango contain no industrially produced trans fats; naturally occurring trans fats from ruminant sources are absent as this is a fully plant-based product). 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients and no stated B12 fortification). 4. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision for a blended estimate. 5. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision for a blended estimate. 6. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision for a blended estimate. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision for a blended estimate. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision for a blended estimate. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported and oat milk fortification with vitamin D varies by brand and is not confirmed for OATSIDE in approved sources. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision. 11. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision. 12. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision. 13. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision. 14. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data at sufficient precision. 15. GI set to 55 (low) based on analogous-food weighted estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (mango GI ~51) and Wolever et al. 2003 (oat milk GI ~58); this is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement. 16. Glycemic load per serving = (55 × 40.8) / 100 = 22.4, where 40.8g carbohydrate = 10.2 g/100g × 400g serving. 17. The description notes that sugar-free sweetener may be substituted when available; default preparation uses cane sugar. Nutritional values reflect the default cane-sugar preparation. 18. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
